title: Camera Operators, Television, Video, and Film
description: Operate television, video, or film camera to record images or scenes for television, video, or film productions.

tasks.text: Compose and frame each shot, applying the technical aspects of light, lenses, film, filters, and camera settings to achieve the effects sought by directors.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate television or motion picture cameras to record scenes for television broadcasts, advertising, or motion pictures.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Adjust positions and controls of cameras, printers, and related equipment to change focus, exposure, and lighting.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Confer with directors, sound and lighting technicians, electricians, and other crew members to discuss assignments and determine filming sequences, desired effects, camera movements, and lighting requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Operate zoom lenses, changing images according to specifications and rehearsal instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe sets or locations for potential problems and to determine filming and lighting requirements.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and perform live shots for broadcast.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Use cameras in any of several different camera mounts, such as stationary, track-mounted, or crane-mounted.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test, clean, maintain, and repair broadcast equipment, including testing microphones, to ensure proper working condition.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Edit video for broadcast productions, including non-linear editing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Instruct camera operators regarding camera setups, angles, distances, movement, and variables and cues for starting and stopping filming.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble studio sets and select and arrange cameras, film stock, audio, or lighting equipment to be used during filming.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read and analyze work orders and specifications to determine locations of subject material, work procedures, sequences of operations, and machine setups.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: View films to resolve problems of exposure control, subject and camera movement, changes in subject distance, and related variables.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Direct studio productions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Set up cameras, optical printers, and related equipment to produce photographs and special effects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read charts and compute ratios to determine variables such as lighting, shutter angles, filter factors, and camera distances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and operate electric news gathering (ENG) microwave vehicles to gather and edit raw footage on location to send to television affiliates for broadcast.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write new scripts for broadcasts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design graphics for studio productions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Stay current with new technologies in the field by reading trade magazines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
